findings.description: json.loads(data) called without try/except wrapper — raw bus data parsed without validation. Malformed JSON will crash the audit trail recorder.
findings.suggested_fix: Wrap in try/except json.JSONDecodeError and log the error.

findings.description: json.loads(data) called without local error handling — raw HTTP response parsed without validation. Network errors or malformed responses will crash the overseer daemon.
findings.suggested_fix: Wrap in try/except (json.JSONDecodeError, ValueError) and return empty dict on failure.

findings.description: json.load(open(wf)) — open() without encoding parameter. Relies on system default encoding (cp1252 on Windows). Will fail on files containing non-ASCII UTF-8 characters.
with open(BOOT_CONFIG) as f — missing encoding parameter for JSON config file reading.
with open(workers_path) as f — reads workers.json without explicit encoding.
with open(workers_path, 'r') as f — reads workers.json without explicit encoding. Multiple instances in this file (lines 710, 744, 754).
findings.suggested_fix: Add encoding='utf-8' to all open() calls reading/writing JSON.

findings.description: with open(path) as f — reads settings JSON without explicit encoding.
Hardcoded path 'D:\\ML' in docstring example. Not a runtime risk but indicates potential hardcoded assumptions.
findings.suggested_fix: Use placeholder path in documentation: '<project_dir>' instead of literal paths.

findings.description: c.eval(tab_id, ...) — Chrome DevTools Protocol JS evaluation. 9 instances across _install_ext_socials.py and ghost_mouse.py. NOT Python eval() — these execute JavaScript in the browser via CDP. Safe by design but should have input sanitization for user-provided values.
findings.suggested_fix: Ensure all JS strings passed to c.eval() are constructed from trusted internal values, never raw user input.

findings.description: Credentials loaded exclusively from environment variables (AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY, CF_API_KEY, SMTP_PASSWORD). No hardcoded secrets found. Clean implementation.
findings.suggested_fix: No action needed — this is the correct pattern.

findings.description: PBKDF2-HMAC-SHA256 with 100K iterations for key derivation. Industry-standard implementation. No hardcoded keys or passwords.
findings.suggested_fix: No action needed — implementation follows best practices.

findings.description: All HTTP servers bind to 127.0.0.1 (localhost only). Files: skynet_agent_telemetry.py, skynet_consultant_bridge.py, skynet_daemon_health_dashboard.py, skynet_dashboard_api.py. NOT exposed to network. No authentication needed for localhost-only services.
findings.suggested_fix: No action needed — localhost binding is sufficient for single-machine deployment.

findings.description: Zero instances of subprocess with shell=True found across all tools/*.py files. All subprocess calls use shell=False (the safe default).
findings.suggested_fix: No action needed — subprocess usage follows security best practices.

findings.description: Zero instances of Python eval() or exec() found. All 'eval' hits are CDP c.eval() calls (Chrome DevTools JS execution), not Python code evaluation.

recommendations: Priority 1: Fix SEC-001 and SEC-002 (missing input validation) — these can crash daemons on malformed data.
Priority 2: Fix SEC-003 through SEC-007 (open without encoding) — systematic sweep to add encoding='utf-8' to all JSON file operations.
Priority 3: No urgent action needed for low/info findings.
